page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture and is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The instance utilizes dedicated CPU allocation, ensuring consistent compute performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable configurations. However, because local storage is absent, all storage operations must rely on external network-attached block storage. Additionally, the CPU flags for advanced vector extensions, matrix multiplication, cryptography, and virtualization are not enabled or reported as active on this specific profile, which may limit acceleration for specialized cryptographic or machine learning workloads.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a balanced resource density suitable for standard workloads without the premium cost associated with memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The c4a-standard-4 is designed for general-purpose applications, including mid-tier web serving, application servers, microservices, and development environments that require dedicated compute resources but do not demand high-performance local disk I/O or hardware acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance within the c4a family, configured with 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. With dedicated CPU allocation, the c4a-standard-4 avoids the performance variability of shared-core environments, though it lacks advanced CPU instruction flags for specialized cryptographic or matrix operations. Qualitatively, it offers a balanced resource profile for standard workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications such as application hosting, web serving, microservices, and software development environments that require consistent compute performance without specialized hardware dependencies.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated compute resources and a standard one-to-four processor-to-memory ratio.

16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Zero integrated GPUs and no complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and microservices
